food_subgroup: Fast food sharing meal — boneless fried chicken wings with sides and beverages

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general chicken wing food product class; no specific entry for boneless fried chicken wing combo meal. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKM covers cooked chicken meat broadly. No specific boneless fried chicken wing combo code exists; closest parent match used for the dominant protein component.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for a fast-food boneless chicken wing combo meal. Omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.description: Full meal for 2 persons: 14 boneless wings, 3 regular fries, 2 ranch dips, 2 drinks
serving.natural_language_portion: One full Meals for 2 Boneless set at Wingstop Singapore comprises 14 boneless chicken wings (two sauce flavors), three regular portions of seasoned french fries, two ranch dipping sauces, and two fountain drinks, totalling approximately 1,400 g of food and beverage combined.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Chicken, wing, frozen, glazed, reheatable, cooked (NDB 21421) and Potatoes, french fried, all types, salt added in processing, frozen, oven-heated (NDB 11413); composite estimation for full combo
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for a Wingstop Meals for 2 Boneless combo. Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted composition: (a) boneless fried chicken wing pieces approximated from USDA FoodData Central entry for breaded fried chicken (FDC ID 331960, 'Chicken, nuggets, cooked, NS as to cooking method') and USDA SR Legacy 'Chicken, wing, frozen, glazed'; (b) french fries from USDA FoodData Central 'Potatoes, french fried, all types, salt added, frozen, oven-heated'; (c) ranch dip from USDA FoodData Central 'Salad dressing, ranch dressing, regular'; (d) soft drink contribution excluded from per-100g solid-food calculation. Per-100g values represent the blended edible solid portion (wings + fries + ranch) weighted by approximate mass fractions: wings ~700g, fries ~450g, ranch ~50g, total solids ~1200g. Beverage (~200g per drink × 2 = 400g) excluded from per-100g macro calculation as it dilutes solid-food nutrient density without adding meaningful macros to the food portion. Confidence: medium.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition; analogous estimate based on 'fried potato (french fries)' GI ~63–70 and 'breaded fried chicken' GI ~55–60, weighted composite
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a Wingstop boneless wings combo meal. GI estimated as weighted composite: french fries GI ~65 (Atkinson et al. 2021, glucose reference, multiple entries 63–70); breaded fried chicken GI ~55 (Atkinson et al. 2021 analogous entry); ranch dip GI ~15 (negligible carbohydrate). Mass-weighted composite GI ≈ 65 (medium). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (carbohydrate_g per serving × GI) / 100 = (28.2 g available carbohydrate per 100g × 1200g solid portion / 100 × 65) / 100 ≈ 18.3 GL units for the full solid meal portion shared by 2 persons.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~520 mg per 100g; estimated ~6,240 mg per full meal for 2)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this meal due to its very high sodium load from seasoned breading, wing sauces, and salted fries, which can significantly exceed the WHO daily sodium limit of 2,000 mg in a single sitting.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium-to-high glycemic index (~65), high refined carbohydrate load from breaded coating and french fries, and added sugars in wing sauces
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise caution with this meal as the combination of medium GI breaded chicken, high-GI french fries, and sugary wing sauces produces a substantial glycemic load that can cause postprandial blood glucose spikes; portion control and sauce selection are critical.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (~2.8 g/100g), trans fat (~0.2 g/100g), high sodium, and deep-fried preparation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this meal due to its deep-fried preparation contributing saturated and trans fats, combined with very high sodium content, both of which are established risk factors for adverse cardiovascular outcomes.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Wingstop Meals for 2 Boneless is a fast-food sharing combo sold at Wingstop restaurants in Singapore (including City Square Mall and Funan outlets) and available via field survey. The meal comprises 14 boneless chicken wing pieces tossed in two customer-selected sauce flavors, three regular portions of Wingstop's signature seasoned french fries, two ranch dipping sauces, and two fountain drinks. Boneless wings are made from whole chicken breast meat cut into wing-sized pieces, coated in a seasoned flour breading, and deep-fried until golden and crispy, then tossed in sauces ranging from mild (Original Hot, Mild) to bold (Lemon Pepper, Garlic Parmesan, Hickory Smoked BBQ, Cajun). The texture is crispy on the outside and juicy inside. Nutritionally, this is a calorie-dense, high-sodium, moderate-to-high fat meal with a medium glycemic index driven primarily by the breaded coating and french fries. The meal is certified Halal in Singapore. It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those on gluten-free diets. Clinically, the high sodium content (~520 mg/100g of solid food) and deep-fried preparation make this meal a concern for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. The meal is priced at SGD 32.25 and is designed for sharing between two people.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for a Wingstop Meals for 2 Boneless combo meal. Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted composition using USDA FoodData Central entries for breaded fried chicken, french fries, and ranch dressing. GI is an analogous estimate from Atkinson et al. 2021 peer-reviewed GI tables. Halal status confirmed from Wingstop Singapore's publicly known certification. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach and absence of a direct laboratory analysis or HPB/USDA branded entry for this specific product.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central component entries (breaded fried chicken, french fries, ranch dressing) weighted by approximate mass fractions. No direct laboratory analysis or branded entry in an approved source exists for this specific Wingstop combo. 2. Beverage component (2 fountain drinks, ~400g total) is excluded from per-100g solid-food nutrient calculation to avoid dilution of meaningful food macros; beverage contribution to total meal energy is minor if unsweetened, or adds ~80–150 kcal per drink if sweetened. 3. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central component entries used for this estimation. 4. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not consistently reported across the component entries used. 5. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the primary component entries used. 6. Trans fat (~0.2 g/100g) reflects residual trans fat from commercial frying oils; value is an estimate. 7. GI set to 65 (medium) based on analogous estimate from Atkinson et al. 2021 peer-reviewed GI tables; no direct GI measurement for this product exists. 8. Halal certification applies to Wingstop Singapore outlets as publicly known; individual sauce formulations should be verified. 9. Serving size of 1,400g represents the full combo for 2 persons including beverages; solid food portion is approximately 1,200g. 10. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and water_g are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data and carry medium uncertainty.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft constructed with composite USDA FoodData Central nutrient estimation, peer-reviewed GI source, full multilingual naming for Singapore cuisine category, and all three health context conditions.

Iteration 2: Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases list — all entries confirmed present verbatim. Verified -1 sentinel values documented in notes (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg). Verified no zero core macros requiring justification. Confirmed GI is numeric > 0. Confirmed G6 multilingual compliance for Singaporean fast-food context. Score confirmed 8/8. However, validator rejected due to invalid ontology match_type values.

Iteration 3: Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type and ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent' to align with allowed values ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none'] as per validator feedback. This resolves the g5_ontology_honesty hard gate failure.
